safety_overview.title: Safety Features
safety_overview.content:
Pipeflow is built to keep live routing calm and recoverable. The safety system has two always-visible pillars:
• Safety Modes - control what changes are allowed
• Panic Button - instant mute for emergencies

The toolbar keeps the current mode in view so you can tell your safety state at a glance.
safety_overview.tip: Before a show or recording pass, check the safety card in the toolbar and make sure you're in the mode you expect.

panic_button.title: 🚨 Panic Button
panic_button.content:
The big red button is your emergency stop! Hit it when:

• Feedback screech attacks your ears
• Something starts making unexpected noise
• You need instant silence NOW

Press Space, F9, or click the panic button to mute ALL audio immediately. Press again to restore normal operation.
panic_button.tip: The panic button works instantly - it's designed for emergencies, not regular muting.

safety_mode_normal.title: Normal Mode
safety_mode_normal.content:
In Normal mode, you have full control. You can:

• Create and remove connections
• Adjust volumes
• Organize the patch
• Make any changes you need

This is the default mode for everyday setup and experimentation.
safety_mode_normal.tip: Normal mode is great for setup work. Switch to Read-Only or Stage once the patch is where you want it.

safety_mode_readonly.title: Read-Only Mode
safety_mode_readonly.content:
Read-Only mode lets you observe without risking changes. Perfect for:

• Monitoring a working setup
• Showing someone your configuration
• Preventing accidental modifications

You can still look around, select things, and inspect details - you just can't change routing or volume.
safety_mode_readonly.tip: Think of Read-Only as 'look around safely'.

safety_mode_stage.title: Stage Mode 🎤
safety_mode_stage.content:
Stage mode is your live-session guardrail. It keeps the current patch stable by blocking routing and volume changes while leaving mute available for emergencies.

Use this when you're performing live or recording something important.
safety_mode_stage.tip: If an action is blocked in Stage mode, the status feedback tells you what was blocked and reminds you to switch back to Normal if you need to edit.

routing_lock.title: Safety Card in the Toolbar
routing_lock.content:
The toolbar safety card shows your current mode, what it means, and lets you switch modes without digging through settings.

This makes safety feel like part of the environment instead of a hidden preference.
routing_lock.tip: If you're about to make a change and the toolbar says Read-Only or Stage, switch back to Normal first.
